verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 (Inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 3) is an ER/sarcoplasmic reticulum resident multi-pass TM channel protein. Its canonical localization is the ER membrane, where it faces the cytoplasm and ER lumen — not the extracellular space. The large regulatory domains face the cytosol; the channel pore spans the ER membrane. No substantial, stable outer-leaflet plasma membrane pool is established. Contextual checks: no documented cancer/cell-death-induced outer-leaflet ectodomain display; not restricted to germline/developmental lineages in a surface-displayed form; not a lysosomal TM protein undergoing exocytosis to PM; no documented minority PM pool with extracellular-face exposure; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering to outer leaflet. IP3R3 has been reported at ER-PM junctions and mitochondria-associated membranes, but these are intracellular membrane contacts, not outer-leaflet plasma membrane display. No clinical surface-directed antibody/ADC/CAR-T program targets this protein on the cell surface.
